step_by_step_thinking:
The given evidence does not contain information directly related to gingival retraction cords or their usage in hypertensive patients. The focus of the retrieved data is mainly on various aspects of obstetric practices, complications, and management strategies rather than dental procedures involving gingiva. Therefore, none of the listed options A through C would typically be recommended due to safety concerns in hypertensive individuals who might experience increased blood pressure upon application of vasoconstrictors like those found in retraction cords containing epinephrine or aluminum chloride. Hence, the correct response according to the context presented here is:
\boxed{D} Based on the available medical evidence focused predominantly on obstetric topics unrelated to dentistry or gingival retraction methods, there is no indication supporting the safe use of any of the mentioned retraction cords in hypertensive patients. Thus, the safest option considering hypertension-related risks is to avoid all listed choices.
